methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hermeus, a Series C-stage developer of supersonic and hypersonic aircraft technology, was last valued at $1.0B post-money following $500M in total capital raised. The valuation universe for this segment is sparse: the supersonic and hypersonic sector contains only two public comparables (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS) and one material private peer (Boom Supersonic), limiting traditional peer-median triangulation.

Given the narrow peer set, only the stage-progression method proved applicable. This approach anchors to Hermeus's $1.0B last post-money valuation and applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier observed for Series C-to-subsequent-stage transitions, yielding an implied valuation of $1.7B. The Peer-Median method was not applied due to insufficient comparable density—the sector lacks three or more same-stage or adjacent-stage peers necessary for a statistically meaningful median.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, with $1.7B as the central point. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint. Key uncertainties include the lack of robust sectoral comparables to benchmark against and the early-stage nature of hypersonic commercialization timelines, which create material execution risk not captured in historical stage-step multiples. Additional data on Boom Supersonic's recent valuation or other series-stage frontier aerospace assets would materially improve confidence in this estimate.
